title: Meshy T-Pose Weapon In Hand
expectedVisibleBehavior: The accepted FPS rest-arms T-pose clip resolves without falling back to a Meshy GLB walking clip.
The real sabre mesh is visible and follows the same RightHand -> WeaponR -> WeaponGrip FK ownership as Ready.
The screenshot-visible hilt marker and real hilt remain in the authored hand/grip region rather than drifting to an unrelated socket-only proof point.
acceptance: Route resolves actor and T-pose clip exactly.
Offline renderer rebuilds the generated T-pose clip instead of applying a Meshy GLB fallback clip.
The real sabre mesh is visible.
The hilt/hand visible relationship is asserted directly and is not inferred from hilt-to-socket pinning alone.
Fresh offline visual evidence uses the current cache token before this case can be called user-facing green.
forbiddenProof: deprecated standalone screencap as acceptance evidence
fallback GLB walking clip evidence for the generated T-pose
hilt-to-socket pinning without a screenshot-visible hilt-to-hand-region check
offline green proof that does not expose the screenshot-visible hilt-to-hand relationship
